architecture: x86_64 (AMD EPYC 7713, Zen3) — CPU only, no GPUs
notes: im is a small CPU-first cluster (3 nodes) for MPI/OpenMP computational-chemistry workloads. There are NO GPUs.
Jobs are described in CPU terms: --nodes, --ntasks-per-node (MPI ranks), --cpus-per-task (OpenMP threads), and --mem. Set OMP_NUM_THREADS to match --cpus-per-task for threaded code.
Slurm accounting is NOT enforced (AccountingStorageEnforce=none): no project/account is required to submit and there is no core-time budget or billing. An --account may be given but is optional.
Wall-time limits are effectively unlimited (all partitions show TIMELIMIT=infinite). Still set a sensible --time so jobs are scheduled predictably; the default is 1 hour.
Memory: DefMemPerNode and MaxMemPerNode are UNLIMITED; selection is by cores+memory (CR_CORE_MEMORY). Request --mem when a job needs a specific amount.
Software is provided through Environment Modules (module avail/load/purge). Load an MPI stack (module load openmpi/5.0.7) and an application module in the job script.
There is no container runtime (no Apptainer/Singularity/Docker) and no shared Lustre or dedicated scratch tier — use /home for code and /data for large datasets.
The login node is im1; do not run heavy computation there — submit a Slurm job.

partitions.name: main
partitions.note: default partition for general CPU/MPI work

partitions.name: debug
partitions.note: for quick tests and short debugging runs

compilers.note: GNU toolchain (gcc/gfortran) with OpenMPI; load an openmpi module for mpicc/mpif90/mpirun. Intel/AOCC are not part of the module set as probed.

storage.home: /home/<user> — 500 GB shared (xfs). Code, scripts, small files.
storage.data: /data — 9.1 TB shared (xfs). Large datasets and results.
storage.note: No Lustre, no per-job scratch tier, no $SCRATCH. TMPDIR is unset by default.

accounting.note: AccountingStorageEnforce=none — jobs run without an account; no points/budget. sacctmgr shows associations for information only.
